title: Volume 1 primary teaching text
note: Doctor Volume 1 learner content is aligned to the academy manual Volume 1 — Foundations of Clinical Trichology (HLI | IIIOHR). Faculty moderation should verify that learners distinguish diagnostic reasoning from memorisation, apply evidence tiers without marketing language, and respect that this program does not confer specialty recognition or replace local licensure or supervised procedural training.

title: Module 1 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 1 (sections 1.1–1.5). Emphasise that trichology is applied hair and scalp medicine, not pattern labelling alone. Workshop: map a patient statement to shedding vs thinning vs breakage vs patchy loss before any treatment discussion. Drill evidence tiers using one established, one consensus-evolving, and one investigational example; prohibit equating biological plausibility with proven efficacy.

title: Module 2 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 2 (sections 2.1–2.8). Use diagrams for infundibulum/isthmus/bulge/bulb. Contrast non-scarring (architecture preserved) vs cicatricial (stem niche destruction). For ethnic variation, keep discussion clinical: breakage, traction context, and surgical planning implications—avoid generalisations that are not evidence-linked.

title: Module 3 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 3 (sections 3.1–3.8). Stress local androgen sensitivity vs serum androgens alone. When discussing PRP or devices, frame as heterogeneous evidence (Tier 2–3) and dependent on preparation and context. Close with treatment logic: core medical therapy vs adjuncts vs surgery sequencing.

title: Module 4 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 4 (sections 4.1–4.9). Run live history drills: female diffuse thinning without skipping menstrual, fertility, thyroid, and hyperandrogenism screens. Red-flag review: pain, burning, pustulation, rapid patchy loss, eyebrow loss, systemic symptoms—escalate biopsy or specialist referral per local protocol. Assess documentation for uncertainty phrased supportively.

title: Volume 2 primary teaching text
note: Doctor Volume 2 learner content is aligned to the academy manual Volume 2 — Diagnostic Trichology and Pattern Hair Loss Medicine (HLI | IIIOHR). Faculty moderation should verify comparative trichoscopy reasoning, phenotype-led investigation discipline, reproductive safety in female pattern medicine, and realistic surgical framing without overclaiming regenerative therapies.

title: Module 5 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 5 (sections 5.1–5.5). Workshop: timed one-minute global inspection drill; then density versus calibre sorting using patient language. Photography: require a written eight-view checklist before accepting learner portfolios. Red-flag rapid-fire: ostia loss, pain, pustules, systemic symptoms.

title: Module 6 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 6 (sections 6.1–6.7). Teach comparator-first discipline (affected vs occipital). Image atlas exercises for pattern loss, TE, AA, and scarring margins. Biopsy targeting: mark advancing margin on a clinical photo before learners choose site.

title: Module 7 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 7 (sections 7.1–7.7). Case-based panels: phenotype-led test selection vs reflex panels. Discuss ferritin and thyroid interpretation with real comorbid contexts. Pathology handoff: require a one-paragraph clinical letter template with differentials and trichoscopy summary.

title: Module 8 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 8 (sections 8.1–8.7). Role-play stabilisation counselling and refusal documentation. Surgical triage: donor miniaturisation, diffuse unpatterned thinning, and juvenile hairline demands. Regenerative framing: PRP heterogeneity; exosomes as emerging only.

title: Module 9 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 9 (sections 9.1–9.8). Drill hyperandrogenism screens without over-testing every FPHL patient. Reproductive safety: contraception reliability and teratogenic meds. Surgery caution in diffuse patterns; prioritise stabilisation and compassionate language for chronic disease.

title: Volume 3 primary teaching text
note: Doctor Volume 3 learner content is aligned to the academy manual Volume 3 — Diffuse Shedding, Cicatricial Alopecia, and Scalp Disease (HLI | IIIOHR). Faculty moderation should verify phenotype-led escalation for scarring and infection, cautious framing of immune-modulating therapies, and referral documentation when primary physicians defer biopsy or specialist care.

title: Module 10 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 10 (diffuse shedding). Workshop: timeline reconstruction on a whiteboard; separate club-shed from broken shafts. Drill mixed AGA plus TE cases with comparative trichoscopy. Red-flag review: patchy loss, pain, inflammation—do not anchor on TE.

title: Module 11 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 11 (alopecia areata). Image-based differentials: tinea, traction, trichotillomania. Discuss JAK inhibitors as jurisdiction- and phenotype-dependent; prohibit marketing certainty. Address psychosocial burden and support pathways.

title: Module 12 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 12 (shaft and breakage). Live exam: collect hairs and classify. Cultural sensitivity for styling practices; pair traction counselling with medical assessment for CCCA overlap when suspected.

title: Module 13 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 13 (cicatricial principles). Emphasise urgency and biopsy margin selection. Role-play pathology handoff letters. Transplant timing only after specialist-led quiescence—document.

title: Module 14 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 14 (lymphocytic scarring). FFA versus FPHL drills with hairline and eyebrow clues. CCCA versus traction: avoid blame; prioritise anti-inflammatory care. DLE: systemic review triggers.

title: Module 15 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 15 (neutrophilic scarring). Show tufting and sinus photography. Stress that antibiotics alone may miss destructive inflammation. Multidisciplinary messaging for dissecting cellulitis.

title: Module 16 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 16 (inflammatory and infectious scalp). Product audit exercise. Tinea kerion and scarring risk—time-to-treatment narratives. Reassess primary alopecia labels after inflammation control.

title: Volume 4 primary teaching text
note: Doctor Volume 4 learner content is aligned to the academy manual Volume 4 — Therapeutics, Regenerative Medicine, and Foundations of Hair Restoration Surgery. Faculty moderation should verify evidence-tier therapeutic framing, disciplined off-label counselling for oral minoxidil, cautious positioning of emerging regenerative modalities (e.g., exosomes), transplant candidacy discipline, and advanced FUE governance messaging without overclaiming.

title: Module 17 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 17 (medical management). Workshop: build a treatment ladder that starts with activity and a defined goal, then sequence topical/oral minoxidil and DHT inhibition. Emphasise oral minoxidil as off-label with safety counselling and monitoring discipline. Teach failure analysis: adherence and expected timelines before intensifying. Validate careful reproductive framing in women.

title: Module 18 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 18 (systemic support). Reinforce the systemic lens: identify whether internal drivers accelerate/amplify/perpetuate hair disorder. Teach ferritin and iron interpretation with clinical context and inflammation awareness. Avoid supplement marketing: only correct proven deficiencies and address modifiable lifestyle/adherence and stress-to-mechanism translation.

title: Module 19 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 19 (regenerative medicine). Teach evidence-tier clarity: PRP as supportive but variably standardised; exosomes as emerging/investigational. Require governance language: document protocol details and track outcomes with baseline photos/trichoscopy. Prevent marketing-driven overclaiming or replacing disease control with regenerative shopping.

title: Module 20 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 20 (transplant foundations). Role-play candidacy discipline: stable loss, quiescence requirements in scarring alopecia, realistic forecasting, and donor reserve constraints. Emphasise surgery as a component of lifelong management including shock loss and ongoing progression. Teach informed refusal-to-proceed and long-view counselling.

title: Module 21 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 21 (advanced FUE science). Workshop: transection-risk reasoning and how decisions adapt to hair/scalp variables. Drill donor distribution governance to prevent overharvesting and moth-eaten depletion. Teach graft stewardship workflow discipline: excellent implantation cannot rescue poor extraction/holding. Reinforce density allocation logic and naturalness restraint with a safe bridge into advanced techniques later.

title: Volume 5 primary teaching text
note: Doctor Volume 5 learner content is aligned to the academy manual Volume 5 — Advanced Surgical Integration, Professional Practice, Research, and Certification. Faculty moderation should verify evidence-tier restraint for advanced innovation topics, practical complication and repair governance, ethical consultation boundaries, research-appraisal discipline, and defensible standards architecture for final certification decisions.

title: Module 22 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 22 (Long Hair FUE and unshaven techniques). Focus on indication discipline over marketing appeal. Drill female and eyebrow pathway selection, concealment trade-offs, and staged planning when full coverage is unsafe. Require donor-governance documentation in all long-hair case discussions.

title: Module 23 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 23 (partial transection and regenerative donor concepts). Enforce disciplined tone: biologic plausibility with explicit uncertainty and emerging evidence boundaries. Teach protocol transparency, informed consent language for innovation, and mandatory outcome-audit fields.

title: Module 24 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 24 (recovery and complications). Train recovery timeline literacy and red-flag escalation triggers. Repair teaching should remain forensic: map design, donor, graft-survival, progression, and expectation factors before re-intervention. Require objective follow-up documentation.

title: Module 25 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 25 (consultation ethics and psychological care). Use scenario-based role-play for pressure demands, age-inappropriate design requests, and refusal-to-proceed communication. Evaluate process-based consent quality, not form completion.

title: Module 26 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 26 (research literacy). Require bias-check walkthroughs and before/after critique checklists in tutorial discussions. Emphasize registry-thinking and protocol/outcome logging as patient-safety and quality tools.

title: Module 27 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 27 (capstone integration). Viva drills should test differential exclusion, sequencing logic, escalation thresholds, and uncertainty language under challenge. Mark against integrated reasoning quality rather than confidence of delivery.

title: Module 28 — faculty delivery notes
note: Source: Manual Module 28 (final assessment and certification). Reinforce multi-domain threshold logic, moderation documentation, and standards-integrity governance for borderline pass/remediation decisions. Certification credibility should be tied to explicit architecture, not single-score shortcuts.
